title: 5 Alcohol Industry Stocks Poised to Counter Adverse Trends

title: Buy Low, Sell High: 7 Blue-Chip Stocks to Snatch Up
text: With the stock market slump since the start of August, investors have new opportunities to buy several leading blue-chip stocks at bargain prices. Many well-known companies and household names had share prices fall 10% or more since mid-summer, when all three major U.S. indices declined on renewed worries of sticky inflation, elevated interest rates, and geopolitical instability.

title: Down -7.02% in 4 Weeks, Here's Why Constellation Brands (STZ) Looks Ripe for a Turnaround

title: Constellation Brands: Mixed Prospects
text: Constellation Brands has seen modest growth this year and benefited from Bud Light's mishaps, as valuations are fair. The company's long-term success was hindered by the pandemic, but it has shown signs of recovery. Shares have traded in a range since February, and the company has made bolt-on deals and undergone a board shake-up.

title: Constellation Brands Beer Sales Soar, but Wine and Spirit Demand Slumps
text: Constellation Brands (STZ) posted better-than-expected quarterly results and raised its guidance as its beer business benefitted from the backlash faced by rival Anheuser-Busch Inbev (BUD) over its partnership with transgender influencer Dylan Mulvaney. However, concerns about Constellation's other liquor offerings sent shares lower.

title: Constellation Brands: Budget-Strapped Consumers Are Buying Less Alcohol, More Often
text: As consumers deal with inflation and other economic challenges, Constellation Brands is seeing shoppers make more frequent alcohol purchases but buying less per trip. title: Modelo parent boosts outlook after Bud Light controversy

title: Constellation Brands has ‘brighter days ahead' after Q2 earnings
text: Constellation Brands Inc (NYSE: STZ) lost about 2.0% today even though it reported market-beating results for its fiscal second quarter. Constellation saw weakness in wine and spirits The stock is trending down perhaps because investors are focusing on a 14% annualised decline in its wine and spirits sales to $444.1 million.

title: Modelo and Corona owner Constellation earnings boosted by strong beer sales
text: Modelo and Corona parent Constellation Brands (NYSE:STZ) reported better than expected sales, nonetheless for its second quarter, with revenue reported at $2.84bn. The revenue figure exceeded analyst forecasts pitched at $2.82 billion.

title: Constellation Brands Stock Brushes Off Beat-and-Raise
text: Constellation Brands, Inc. (NYSE:STZ ) reported fiscal second-quarter profits of $3.70 per share and $2.84 billion in revenue before the bell, both of which bested analysts' estimates.

title: Constellation Brands (STZ) Q2 Earnings Surpass Estimates
text: Constellation Brands (STZ) came out with quarterly earnings of $3.70 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $3.38 per share. This compares to earnings of $3.17 per share a year ago.

title: Constellation Brands Raises Profit Estimate on Strong Modelo Sales
text: Constellation Brands expects fiscal 2024 adjusted earnings of between $12 and $12.20 a share.

title: Constellation Brands' stock sinks 2% after drinks giant offers guidance that lags conensus
text: Constellation Brands Inc.'s stock STZ, +0.41% fell 2% in premarket trade Thursday, after the distributor of Corona beer and Kim Crawford wines posted better-than-expected earnings for its fiscal second quarter but offered guidance that lagged consensus by a wide margin. The company posted net income of $690.0 million, or $3.74 a share, for the quarter to Aug. 31, after a loss of $1.15 billion, or $6.30 a share, in the year-earlier quarter.

title: Constellation Brands Reports Second Quarter Fiscal 2024 Financial Results
text: VICTOR, N.Y., Oct. 05, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Constellation Brands, Inc. (NYSE: STZ), a leading beverage alcohol company, reported today its second quarter fiscal 2024 financial results. A conference call to discuss the financial results and outlook will be hosted by President and Chief Executive Officer, Bill Newlands, and Chief Financial Officer, Garth Hankinson, on Thursday, October 5, 2023 at 10:30 a.m. EDT. Visit ir.cbrands.com to locate information for joining the conference call or a live, listen-only webcast of the conference call.
